rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to component ports.
Use AGREE annex syntax with `annex agree {** ... **};` delimiters and valid contract elements like `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const`.
generic_rules: Stateful equations must use initialized temporal recurrence; branching logic must use nested `if ... then ... else` expressions.
Generated AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and only reference visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Helper functions and constants should match the data types of their referenced variables or be imported from available packages.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for behavioral contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for `assign` clauses.
Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related AADL tokens not automatically in AGREE scope
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
Example-specific names or scenario details from RAG cards
